description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki:
Florida (  FLORR-ih-də; Spanish: [floˈɾiða] ) is a state in the Southeastern and South Atlantic
regions of the United States.

description: flooded grassland in southern Florida, United States
description_wiki: The Everglades is a natural region of flooded grasslands in the southern portion of the U.S. state of Florida, comprising the southern half of a large drainage basin within the Neotropical realm.
Cabbage, comprising several cultivars of Brassica oleracea, is a leafy green, red (purple), or white (pale green) biennial plant grown as an annual vegetable crop for its dense-leaved heads.

description: group of venomous snakes of the genera Crotalus and Sistrurus
description_wiki: Rattlesnakes are venomous snakes that form the genera Crotalus and Sistrurus of the subfamily Crotalinae (the pit vipers).

description: unincorporated community in southeastern Highlands County, Florida, United States
description_wiki: Venus is an unincorporated community in southeastern Highlands County, Florida, United States.

description: state highway in Escambia County, Florida, United States
description_wiki: State Road 97 (SR 97) is a south-north two-lane highway entirely in Escambia County, Florida.

description: youth wing of U.S. Democratic Party
description_wiki: The Young Democrats of America (YDA) is the youth wing of the Democratic Party of the United States.

description: county in Florida, United States of America
description_wiki: Highlands County is a county located in the Florida Heartland region of the U.S. state of Florida.

aliases: Independence Day in the United States
description: United States holiday held on July 4 to mark the Declaration of Independence
description_wiki: Independence Day, known colloquially as the Fourth of July, is a federal holiday in the United States which commemorates the adoption of the Declaration of Independence on July 4, 1776, establishing the United States of America.

description: United States government New Deal agency (1935–1939) employing millions of unemployed people to carry out public works projects
description_wiki: The Works Progress Administration (WPA; from 1935 to 1939, then known as the Work Projects Administration from 1939 to 1943) was an American New Deal agency that employed millions of jobseekers (mostly men who were not formally educated) to carry out public works projects, including the construction of public buildings and roads.
